title: Specify idempotency for unsafe operations
description: Define how clients can safely retry writes, typically with an idempotency key, so a retried request never produces a duplicate effect.
body: Use when designing create or other non-idempotent operations clients may retry. Do not leave retry behavior undefined for writes. Actions: accept an idempotency key on unsafe operations; specify that a repeated key returns the original result; document the key's scope and retention window. Validation signals include a retried create returning the first result with no duplicate resource created. Risk boundary: undefined retry semantics cause duplicate orders or charges on network retries, so make idempotency an explicit part of the contract and defer money movement to the finance domain.
